rationale: In the citing text, this reference is not just listed as general background; it is tied to a specific theoretical claim in hypothesis development. The authors cite Nikolova and Inman (2015) to justify expected differential processing of nutrition information across category types. That cited finding is carried into the citing paper's conceptual model and empirical tests of category-level moderation, even though the paper does not reuse the cited study's design or data.
relation_description: The club-store paper uses Nikolova and Inman (2015) as prior evidence that consumers attend more to nutrition information in categories with a positive health halo. It applies that insight to build its own moderation prediction that perceived category healthfulness should shape the club-store effect on nutritional quality outcomes.
